data.title: Polls show Trump's disapproval rating has risen to 59%, a record high during his presidency.
data.description: A recent poll released by Fox News shows that approximately 59% of American voters surveyed are dissatisfied with President Trump's administration, an 8-percentage-point increase from a year ago and a new high during his term; the approval rating, however, has fallen from 49% to 41%. The survey was based on a random sample of 1,001 registered voters. Among different parties, Republican voters' approval rating for Trump is 84%, a decrease of 8 percentage points year-over-year; while the disapproval ratings for Democrats and independent voters are 95% and 75%, respectively. (Xinhua)

data.title: U.S. Voter Satisfaction with President Trump Declines, Fox News Poll Reveals
data.description:
A recent poll conducted by Fox News indicates that approximately 59% of American voters are dissatisfied with U.S. President Donald Trump's performance, marking an 8% increase from a year ago and reaching a new high during his tenure. According to Odaily, the satisfaction rate has decreased from 49% to 41%.

The survey was based on a random sample of 1,001 registered voters. Among different political affiliations, Republican voters showed an 84% satisfaction rate with Trump, which is an 8% decrease compared to the previous year. Meanwhile, dissatisfaction among Democratic and independent voters reached 95% and 75%, respectively.

data.title: Petron Corp. Secures Russian Crude Oil Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Petron Corp., the sole oil refiner in the Philippines, has acquired 2.48 million barrels of crude oil from Russia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of efforts to bolster the country's energy supply amidst the ongoing conflict in Iran. The procurement aims to ensure stability in meeting domestic energy demands during this period of geopolitical tension.

data.title: Ethereum Dominates Global Tokenized Asset Settlement
data.description: Ethereum currently handles 61.4% of the world's tokenized assets, according to PANews. Data from Token Terminal indicates that Ethereum has settled tokenized assets valued at $206.2 billion, marking a year-over-year growth of over 40%.

data.title: Wall Street Strategists Suggest Trades Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: As the conflict in Iran enters its fifth week, Wall Street bank strategists are recommending trades that could benefit from a gradual stock-market decline. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these strategies are designed to capitalize on a slow and steady market selloff. The ongoing geopolitical tensions have prompted financial experts to reassess market dynamics and suggest investment approaches that align with the current environment. Investors are advised to consider these strategies as part of their broader portfolio management during this period of uncertainty.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 610 USDT with a 0.88% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 29, 2026, 14:04 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 610 USDT and is now trading at 609.919983 USDT, with a narrowed 0.88% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Prediction Markets See Significant Growth in Cryptocurrency Trading Volume
data.description: Prediction markets have experienced substantial growth in the cryptocurrency sector, accounting for 2.47% of the spot trading volume in March. According to NS3.AI, this marks a significant increase from the 0.11% share recorded in March of the previous year, representing more than a 20-fold growth and setting a new record high.

data.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $70,000 Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has extended the pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure until April 6. According to NS3.AI, this decision followed a March 23 announcement that briefly pushed Bitcoin's price above $70,000. The announcement also contributed to a $1.7 trillion increase in U.S. stock market value and a 15% decline in oil prices.

Iranian officials, however, denied any direct negotiations with the U.S., labeling the claims as fake news and psychological warfare. Following Iran's denial, oil prices rebounded, U.S. stocks lost approximately half of their gains, and Bitcoin's price retreated. Additionally, $265 million in cryptocurrency short positions were liquidated within 15 minutes.

data.title: U.S. Treasury's $69 Billion 2-Year Note Auction Sees Weaker Demand
data.description: The U.S. Treasury conducted an auction on Tuesday, selling $69 billion of 2-year notes at a high yield of 3.936%. According to NS3.AI, demand for these notes was weaker compared to the previous month, with the bid-to-cover ratio decreasing to 2.44 from February's 2.63. Primary dealers ended up taking a larger share of the sale. This weaker auction occurred amid rising oil prices due to Middle East conflicts and diminishing hopes for swift Federal Reserve rate cuts.

data.title: CoinList to Support OneFootball's OFC Token Generation Event
data.description: CoinList announced on the X platform that it will support the OFC token generation event for the football media platform OneFootball on April 9. According to ChainCatcher, the tokens will be distributed to the non-custodial CoinList wallets of participants in the sale.

data.title: Significant Bitcoin Transfer Between Anonymous Addresses
data.description: A substantial Bitcoin transaction has been reported involving anonymous addresses. According to ChainCatcher, at 21:08, a total of 473.5 BTC was transferred from one anonymous address, starting with bc1qq5je, to another, beginning with bc1q4w6.

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Positions Reach $3.525 Billion
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whales currently hold positions totaling $3.525 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.756 billion, representing 49.8% of the total, while short positions amount to $1.77 billion, making up 50.2%. Long positions have incurred a loss of $116 million, whereas short positions have gained $137 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has taken a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2,148.7, resulting in an unrealized loss of $10.353 million.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Xiaomi Launches MiMo-V2-Pro AI Model with Competitive Pricing
data.description: Xiaomi has introduced its latest AI model, MiMo-V2-Pro, on March 18. According to NS3.AI, this model features over one trillion parameters and is priced at $1 per million input tokens and $3 per million output tokens. MiMo-V2-Pro has achieved an eighth-place ranking on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and scored 78% on the SWE-bench Verified. While hands-on tests have shown strong performance in coding and creative writing, the model exhibits weaker capabilities in frontier-math tasks.

data.title: Aave Governance Dispute Intensifies Amid v4 Upgrade Preparations
data.description: Aave is currently experiencing a governance dispute concerning the allocation of product revenue back to the DAO as it gears up for its v4 upgrade. According to NS3.AI, the conflict escalated following Aave Labs' proposal of "Aave Will Win" in February. The situation further intensified when the governance group ACI announced in early March that it would cease operations due to disagreements with the developer firm.

data.title: Expert Warns of Risks in Outsourcing Bitcoin Custody
data.description: Kevin Loaec has highlighted potential risks for institutions outsourcing Bitcoin custody. According to NS3.AI, Loaec pointed out that relying on pooled custody, capped or conditional insurance, and vendor dependence could expose clients to systemic failures. He emphasized that policy-driven wallets could enforce governance on-chain, potentially mitigating some of these risks.

data.title: El Salvador's Bitcoin reserves have increased to 7,605.37, worth approximately $506 million.
data.description: El Salvador's official agency, The Bitcoin Office, announced on the X platform that the country's strategic Bitcoin reserve holdings have increased to 7,605.37 Bitcoins, worth approximately $506 million.

data.title: El Salvador's Bitcoin Reserves Reach New Milestone
data.description: El Salvador's official entity, The Bitcoin Office, announced on the X platform that the country's Bitcoin strategic reserves have surpassed 7,600 BTC. According to ChainCatcher, the reserves have increased to 7,605.37 BTC. The value of these holdings is approximately $506 million.

data.title: Ethereum's Market Position Threatened by Stablecoin Growth
data.description:
Ether's (ETH) dominance as the second-largest cryptocurrency is under threat, not due to its proximity to Bitcoin (BTC), but because of the rapid expansion of the stablecoin market. According to Cointelegraph, Ether's position is being challenged as Tether (USDT) and other stablecoins experience significant growth. Over the past five years, Ether has lagged behind top stablecoins like USDT and USD Coin (USDC) in terms of market capitalization growth. While ETH's market cap increased by approximately 11.75% to around $240 billion, USDT saw a staggering 622.50% growth, reaching over $184 billion. This trend has led to speculation about Ethereum's potential loss of its number-two spot by 2026.

On platforms like Polymarket, a significant number of traders are betting on Ethereum being overtaken by 2026, with over 59% of participants predicting this outcome, a notable increase from 17% at the start of the year. The differing growth trajectories of Ethereum and Tether can be attributed to their distinct natures; Ethereum's value is heavily reliant on the price of ETH, which has struggled amid macroeconomic pressures such as U.S. tariffs, geopolitical tensions, and shifting Federal Reserve policies. This has also impacted institutional interest, with U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs experiencing a 65% drop in assets under management, falling to $11.76 billion in March from $31.86 billion in October of the previous year.

In contrast, Tether's growth is fueled by capital inflows into stablecoins, which are seen as safer, more liquid alternatives during volatile market conditions. The total stablecoin market has surged to $310 billion from just $5 billion in 2020, with Tether holding a 58% share. This "dry powder" effect, where capital is parked in dollar-pegged assets, remains strong during risk-off periods. For Ethereum to regain momentum, a stronger risk appetite is necessary to boost ETH's price, whereas Tether benefits from investor caution. This dynamic explains why ETH's market cap growth has lagged behind USDT, despite Ethereum's foundational role in the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

Looking ahead to 2026, Ether faces potential price declines from a technical standpoint. As of Sunday, ETH was trading within a "bear flag" pattern, which suggests a higher likelihood of further downward movement if the price breaks below the pattern's lower trendline. Should this occur, ETH's price could fall towards the pattern's downside target of approximately $1,250 by June, assuming the breakdown continues.

data.title: Trump's Ultimatum to Iran May Increase Oil Prices and Market Volatility
data.description: Garret Jin, the agent for the '1011 Insider Whale,' indicated that U.S. President Donald Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran regarding passage through the Strait of Hormuz could lead to higher oil prices and increased market volatility. According to NS3.AI, Jin noted that the crude oil volatility index OVX has risen to 93, while the CBOE Volatility Index VIX is approximately 24, resulting in a ratio close to four times.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Cathie Wood Highlights AI's Potential in Healthcare
data.description: Cathie Wood, founder of Ark Invest, emphasized the underestimated potential of artificial intelligence in healthcare during a recent interview. According to PANews, Wood believes AI can revolutionize cancer diagnosis through blood tests before symptoms appear. She also noted that the pharmaceutical industry faces a $300 billion patent cliff over the next five years, presenting both challenges and opportunities.
